---
title: "App and Document"
subtitle: "The two-class v2 facade"
---

# The split

hwpapi v2 divides the public surface into two top-level classes:

| Class | Owns | Obtained from |
|---|---|---|
| [`App`](../reference/App.qmd) | COM engine lifecycle, window visibility, open/save/close/quit | `App()` |
| [`Document`](../reference/Document.qmd) | Everything scoped to *one* open document — text, cursor, collections, page layout | `app.doc` |

This is the central design choice of v2. The v1 `App` carried 82+
public members; the v2 `App` has **≤ 15**. Every document-scoped
member moved to `Document`.

# When to use which

- **App** — connect/disconnect, open a file, save or export, toggle
  window visibility, reach the escape hatch.
- **Document** (`app.doc`) — read/write text, navigate the cursor,
  insert things, access collections (`fields`, `bookmarks`, `images`,
  …), configure page layout.

```{python}
#| eval: false
from hwpapi import App

app = App()

# Lifecycle on App
app.open("report.hwp")
app.save()
app.save_as("report.pdf")

# Content on app.doc
text = app.doc.text
app.doc.insert_text("new paragraph\n")
app.doc.fields["author"] = "홍길동"
app.doc.page.setup(width="210mm", height="297mm")

app.close()
app.quit()
```

# `App` — the lifecycle layer

The full v2 `App` public surface is:

| Member | Kind | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `App(new_app=False, is_visible=True, dll_path=None, engine=None)` | constructor | Connect to HWP |
| `open(path)` | method | Open a `.hwp` / `.hwpx` file |
| `new()` | method | Create a blank document |
| `close()` | method | Close the active document |
| `save()` | method | Save in place |
| `save_as(path, *, as_block=False)` | method | Save to a new file (format inferred from extension) |
| `reload()` | method | Rebind to an existing COM engine |
| `quit()` | method | Tear down the COM engine |
| `visible` | property (r/w) | Window visibility |
| `doc` | property | The active [`Document`](../reference/Document.qmd) |
| `engine` | property | The [`Engine`](../reference/low/engine.qmd) — escape hatch |
| `api` | property | Raw HWP COM handle — ultimate escape hatch |
| `actions` | property | The action catalogue — escape hatch |
| `__enter__` / `__exit__` | context manager | Auto-closes on exit |

Context-manager usage handles cleanup for you:

```{python}
#| eval: false
from hwpapi import App

with App() as app:
    app.open("template.hwp")
    app.doc.insert_text("Generated by hwpapi\n")
    app.save_as("generated.pdf")
# app.close() + app.quit() happen here even if an exception was raised
```

# `Document` — the per-document layer

`app.doc` returns the same `Document` instance on every access
(`@cached_property`). It owns:

- **Text** — `doc.text` (read/write), `doc.get_text()`,
  `doc.insert_text(s)`, `doc.get_selected_text()`, `doc.selection`
- **Navigation** — `doc.cursor.goto_page(n)`, `doc.cursor.move_to_field(name)`,
  `doc.cursor.in_table()`, `doc.cursor.get_charshape()`, `doc.cursor.get_parashape()`
- **Clipboard** — `doc.copy()`, `doc.cut()`, `doc.paste()`, `doc.delete()`
- **Undo** — `doc.undo()`, `doc.redo()`
- **Search** — `doc.find_text(q)`, `doc.replace_all(find, replace)`
- **Structure** — `doc.insert_page_break()`, `doc.insert_line_break()`,
  `doc.insert_paragraph_break()`, `doc.insert_tab()`,
  `doc.insert_heading(level, text)`
- **Inserts** — `doc.insert_file(path)`, `doc.insert_picture(path)`
- **Page setup** — `doc.page.setup(...)`
- **Counters** — `doc.page_count`, `doc.current_page`,
  `doc.get_filepath()`, `doc.get_hwnd()`
- **Collections** (dict-like) — `doc.fields`, `doc.bookmarks`,
  `doc.hyperlinks`, `doc.images`, `doc.styles`, `doc.paragraphs`,
  `doc.tables`
- **Scanner** — `doc.scan(...)` context manager

# Why not merge them?

The v1 `App` was a god-object: 82 public members mixing lifecycle,
content, formatting, navigation, and low-level utilities. The split
serves three goals:

1. **Discoverability.** `dir(app)` now fits on one screen; everything
   else lives under `app.doc.*` with IDE tab completion.
2. **Lifetime.** A `Document` stops being useful when the underlying
   file closes. Splitting the surface makes the invalidation boundary
   explicit — future v2.1 work on multi-document support targets
   `app.documents[…]` returning a new `Document`, without touching
   `App`.
3. **Teachability.** "App = COM engine, Document = contents" is a
   sentence. "App = everything HWP-related" was a paragraph.

# See also

- [Collections guide](collections.qmd) — the dict-like accessors under `app.doc.*`
- [Context scopes](context-scopes.qmd) — `charshape_scope`, `parashape_scope`, `styled_text`
- [Low-level escape hatch](low-level-escape-hatch.qmd) — `hwpapi.low.*`
- [Migration v1 → v2](../getting-started/migration-v1-to-v2.qmd) — full 1:1 table
